title: Piquant Italian Haggis Vitello Tonnato
description: A garlicky, bouncy-qq fusion of roasted veal, pre-cooked haggis, and a tangy tuna‑kimchi sauce, perfect for a lazy Sunday meal.
instructions: Preheat oven to 200C and drizzle the veal with olive oil, then season with salt.
Roast the veal for 15 minutes, then lower the temperature to 180C and cook another 12 minutes until medium-rare.
While the veal cooks, heat a small pan over medium heat and sauté minced garlic in a teaspoon of olive oil until fragrant.
Add the chopped kimchi and cook for 3 minutes to release its flavors.
Transfer the kimchi mixture to a bowl and blend with fresh tuna, capers, anchovy fillets, lemon juice, mustard, and egg yolk.
Blend until smooth, adding a splash of water if the sauce is too thick.
Once the veal reaches the desired doneness, remove it from the oven and let it rest for 5 minutes.
Slice the veal into thin medallions and arrange them on a plate.
Spoon the tuna‑kimchi sauce over the veal slices, ensuring each piece is coated.
Garnish with a drizzle of olive oil and a sprinkle of sea salt, then serve immediately.
